thales triq On the Verification of Timed Discrete-Event Models Christos Stergiou , Stavros Tripakis , Eleftherios Matsikoudis , Edward A. Lee Citation Christos Stergiou, Stavros Tripakis, Eleftherios Matsikoudis, Edward A. Lee. "On the Verification o

Matsikoudis, Edward A. Lee. "On the Verification of Timed Discrete-Event Models". 11th International Conference on Formal Modeling and Analysis of Timed Systems (FORMATS), 29, August, 2013; Buenos Aires, Argentina. Abstract Timed discrete-event (DE)

res, Argentina. Abstract Timed discrete-event (DE) is an actor-oriented formalism for modeling timed systems. A DE model is a network of actors consuming/producing timed events from/to a set of input/output channels. In this paper we study a basic DE

output channels. In this paper we study a basic DE model, called deterministic DE (DDE), where actors are simple constant-delay components, and two extensions of DDE: NDE, where actors are non-deterministic delays, and DETA, where actors are either d

nistic delays, and DETA, where actors are either deterministic delays or timed automata. We investigate verification questions on DE models and examine expressiveness relationships between the DE models and timed automata. Electronic downloads model

els and timed automata. Electronic downloads model_checking.pdf · application/pdf · 347 kbytes http://dl.acm.org/citation.cfm?id=2529517 . (In Proceedings of the 11th international conference on Formal Modeling and Analysis of Timed Systems , pp 213
